problem: Hydrogen and oxygen gas are combined in a reaction vessel and reacted completely to form water. What volumes of gases (at STP) will give the greatest mass of water?

Potassium hydrogen phthalate (KHP, M = 204) is a monoprotic acid used as a primary standard in acid-base titrations. It takes 28.15 mL of a solution of Sr(OH)2 to neutralize 0.402 g KHP. What is the concentration of the Sr(OH)2 solution?
20.0 mL of 0.15 M aqueous Pb(NO3)2 and 10.0 mL of 0.35 M aqueous NaBr are mixed, giving a white precipitate. Which ion is LEAST abundant in the supernatant?

A sample of 9.736 g of a lead oxide is reduced completely with excess carbon monoxide to give metallic lead. The resulting gas is bubbled into saturated lime water, resulting in the precipitation of 6.636 g calcium carbonate (M = 100.09) after drying. What is the formula of this lead oxide?
A mixture of K2S, K2SO3, and K2SO4 is found to contain 60.0% K by mass. What is the mass percent of oxygen in the mixture?

A solution of 5.00 g oxalic acid (H2C2O4, M = 90.04) in 100.0 g H2O has a freezing point of -1.31 degrees C. What is the percent ionization of oxalic acid in this solution? The freezing point depression constant of water is Kf = 1.86 degrees C m^-1.
Which glassware would be most appropriate for measuring 10.00 mL of distilled water?

A solution that may contain either 0.1 M Ag+(aq), 0.1 M Pb2+(aq), or both, is treated with 1 M aqueous HCl. A white precipitate forms which does not appear to dissolve in hot water. Which conclusion about the cations present may be drawn?
choices: Ag+ is present, and Pb2+ may be present.
Pb2+ is present, and Ag+ may be present.

problem: Addition of 10 mL of distilled water to 0.1 g of which salt produces a yellow, slightly cloudy solution?

The amount of ascorbic acid in a vitamin C tablet is determined by titration with a solution of iodine in aqueous potassium iodide. A small amount of starch is added to the vitamin C solution before the titration. What is the function of the starch?
choices: It forms an intensely colored complex with triiodide ion.
It increases the viscosity of the analyte solution.
It catalyzes the dissociation of triiodide into iodine and iodide.
It binds to the inert ingredients of the vitamin C tablet.

problem: When blue solid CuSO4*5 H2O is heated, it turns white. Which is the best explanation for this observation?
choices: Heating causes water molecules bonded to copper to be replaced by sulfate, lowering the energy of the light absorbed.
Heating causes an expansion of the lattice, leading to a decrease in the coordination number of copper and raising the energy of the light absorbed.
Heating causes reduction of copper(II) to copper(I).
Heating causes reduction of sulfate to sulfite.

problem: A student performs an experiment to determine the concentration of a colored salt solution by measuring the absorbance of the solution at the wavelength of maximum absorbance of the salt (lambda_max) and using Beer's Law to calculate the concentration. Which of the following could cause the measured concentration to be higher than the actual concentration?
choices: The cuvette is not rinsed with the salt solution after being washed.
The cuvette is not wiped off before it is inserted into the spectrophotometer.
Less than the recommended volume of salt solution is added to the cuvette.
The spectrometer is set to a wavelength different from lambda_max.

problem: Which C3H6O isomer has the lowest normal boiling point?

Dichloromethane (CH2Cl2, M = 84.93) is a volatile liquid with a vapor pressure of 0.53 atm at 24 degrees C. 13.8 g of dichloromethane is introduced into a 5.0-L evacuated container maintained at 24 degrees C. Which is the best description of the system at equilibrium?
choices: One-third of the CH2Cl2 has evaporated.
Two-thirds of the CH2Cl2 has evaporated.
All of the CH2Cl2 has evaporated to give a pressure of 0.53 atm.
All of the CH2Cl2 has evaporated to give a pressure of 0.79 atm.

problem: A piece of solid CO2 initially at -78 degrees C is placed in a small plastic container, which is then sealed and allowed to warm up in a room whose temperature is 22 degrees C. Ultimately the plastic container explodes, ending any further observations. Which statement best describes the possibility of observing liquid CO2 before the container explodes? (The phase diagram of CO2 is shown below with triple point at 5.2 atm, 217 K and critical point at 73 atm, 304 K.)
choices: Liquid CO2 must be observable, since the explosion is driven by hydrostatic pressure of liquid CO2.
Liquid CO2 may be observed, since the triple point may be reached during this experiment.
Liquid CO2 may not be observed, since only sublimation of solid CO2 is possible under the conditions of the experiment.
Liquid CO2 may not be observed, but supercritical CO2 may be observed.

problem: A solid calcium boride CaBx has a density of 2.45 g cm^-3. It has a primitive cubic lattice with an edge length a = 415 pm and with one calcium atom per unit cell. What is the value of x?

The lattice enthalpy of Na2O is -2600 kJ mol^-1 while the lattice enthalpy of MgO is -3900 kJ mol^-1. Which factors contribute to the larger magnitude of the lattice enthalpy of MgO?
I. Mg2+ has a larger charge than Na+.
II. Mg2+ is smaller than Na+.

A sample of benzene (C6H6) is heated at a constant rate. Which graph most accurately represents how the temperature changes with time? (Graph A shows two plateaus with increasing segments; Graph B shows smooth curve; Graph C shows one plateau; Graph D shows step function)
choices: Graph with two flat plateaus (melting and boiling) connected by rising segments
Graph with smooth continuous rise then plateau then rise
Graph with one plateau then continuous rise
Graph with gradual rise and one plateau

problem: An insulated container holds 100.0 g of water at 25.0 degrees C. A 100.0 g piece of copper at 50.0 degrees C is added to the container. What is the final temperature of the mixture in the container at equilibrium? The specific heat capacity of copper is 0.385 J g^-1 K^-1.
At the triple point of water (0.01 degrees C, 0.006 atm), in which phase would 1.0 mol of water have the lowest entropy?
choices: It would have the same entropy in all three phases.

problem:
The standard enthalpy of formation of NO2(g) is 33.1 kJ mol^-1. What is the average bond dissociation enthalpy of a nitrogen-oxygen bond in NO2(g)?
Bond dissociation enthalpies: N=N: 945.4 kJ mol^-1, O=O: 498.4 kJ mol^-1.

The formation of ammonia from nitrogen and hydrogen has a standard free energy change of -32.8 kJ mol^-1 at 298 K.
N2(g) + 3 H2(g) -> 2 NH3(g)    Delta G degrees = -32.8 kJ mol^-1
What is Delta G at 298 K if the partial pressures of all three gases are 3.00 bar?

What is Kp for the dissociation of PCl5(g) at 298 K?
PCl5(g) -> PCl3(g) + Cl2(g)    Kp = ???
Substance | Delta G degrees_f, kJ mol^-1: PCl5(g) = -325, PCl3(g) = -286

A reaction has Keq = 0.10 at 300 K and Keq = 0.50 at 330 K. Which statements about this reaction in this temperature range must be correct?
I. Delta H degrees_rxn > 0
II. Delta S degrees_rxn > 0

The time course of a reaction in which a reactant R is converted to a product P is shown below. Which statements about the reaction are correct?
I. The reaction is first order in R.
II. Two moles of P are formed for every mole of R that reacts.
(Graph shows R decreasing from ~0.07 M and P increasing to ~0.04 M over time)

In a reaction A + 2 B -> C + D, initial rates were measured as shown in the table. What is the initial rate of the reaction with [A]0 = 0.200 M and [B]0 = 0.400 M?
[A]0=0.120, [B]0=0.300, rate=1.80x10^-5 | [A]0=0.180, [B]0=0.450, rate=4.05x10^-5 | [A]0=0.080, [B]0=0.200, rate=8.00x10^-6
choices: It cannot be determined from the information given.

problem: The temperature-dependence of the rate constants of a series of six related reactions are studied, and the reactions are all found to have the same rate constant at 370 K (their isokinetic temperature). Which statement best describes the relationship between the reactions' Arrhenius pre-factors A and their activation energies Ea?
choices: The reactions share a common pre-factor A but differ in their activation energies Ea.
The reactions share a common activation energy Ea but differ in their pre-factors A.
As the reactions' pre-factors A increase, their activation energies Ea increase.
As the reactions' pre-factors A increase, their activation energies Ea decrease.

problem:
Which properties of the reaction whose reaction coordinate is shown below increase with increasing temperature? (Diagram shows an exothermic reaction with activation energy barrier.)
I. The forward rate constant of the reaction, kf
II. The equilibrium constant of the reaction, Keq

The hydrolysis of formamide to formate ion in basic aqueous solution is proposed to proceed by the following steps:
HCONH2 + OH- <=> HCO(OH)(NH2)- (unfavorable)
HCO(OH)(NH2)- + OH- <=> HCO2(NH2)2- + H2O (fast, unfavorable)
HCO2(NH2)2- -> HCO2- + NH2- (irreversible)
NH2- + H2O -> NH3 + OH- (fast, favorable)
Which of the following statements is necessarily true, given this mechanism?
choices: The reaction is first-order in HCONH2.
The reaction is first-order in hydroxide ion.
HCO2- is an intermediate in this reaction.
If the reaction is carried out in deuterium oxide, the formate ion will contain deuterium.

problem: An irreversible reaction A + B -> C is first-order in both A and B. In a solution with [A]0 = 0.20 M, it takes 37 s for the concentration of B to decrease from its initial value of 0.010 M to 0.0050 M. How long will it take for the concentration of B to decrease from an initial value of 0.010 M to 0.0050 M if the initial concentration of A is 0.010 M?
What is the percent ionization of a 0.10 M solution of nitrous acid (HNO2, Ka = 7.2 x 10^-4)?

The reaction mixture below represents the reaction A2(g) + B2(g) <=> 2 AB(g) with Keq = 2.5. Which statement correctly describes the situation? (Diagram shows molecules of A2, B2, and AB in a container.)
choices: The reaction will shift to produce more products.
The reaction will shift to produce more reactants.
Not enough information is given to make a prediction.

problem:
The hydrogenation of carbon monoxide represented below is exothermic.
CO(g) + 2 H2(g) <=> CH3OH(l)    Delta H degrees_rxn < 0
Which changes will increase the equilibrium yield of CH3OH?
choices: I only (Increasing the temperature of the system)
II only (Removing some of the CH3OH(l))

problem:
To a solution that is 0.010 M each in fluoride, sulfite, and phosphate ions is added dropwise a 1.00 M solution of CaCl2. In what order will the solids precipitate?
Solid | Ksp: CaSO3 = 6.8 x 10^-8, CaF2 = 5.3 x 10^-9, Ca3(PO4)2 = 1.0 x 10^-25
choices: First Ca3(PO4)2, then CaF2, last CaSO3
First Ca3(PO4)2, then CaSO3, last CaF2
First CaSO3, then CaF2, last Ca3(PO4)2
First CaF2, then CaSO3, last Ca3(PO4)2

problem: The graph shows the logarithm of the molar solubility S of an ionic solid as a function of pH. Which solid is it? (Graph shows log S decreasing from pH 1 to about pH 4, then leveling off at higher pH.)

Some solid silver nitrate is dissolved in 100.0 mL of 1.00 M NH3 solution. To this solution, 50.0 mL of 1.00 M NaCl solution must be added to see the first sign of cloudiness. How much AgNO3 was present? The Ksp of AgCl is 1.8 x 10^-10 and the Kf of Ag(NH3)2+ is 1.0 x 10^8.
In basic solution, Cl2(g) disproportionates to form Cl-(aq) and ClO3-(aq). What is the mole ratio of Cl-(aq) to ClO3-(aq) produced in this reaction?
What are the oxidation states of the sulfur atoms in the thiosulfate ion, S2O3^2-?
choices: Both sulfur atoms have an oxidation state of +2.
Both sulfur atoms have an oxidation state of +3.
One sulfur atom has an oxidation state of +5 and one has an oxidation state of -1.
One sulfur atom has an oxidation state of +6 and one has an oxidation state of -2.

problem: A new electrode material is being tested for its ability to selectively reduce oxygen gas to hydrogen peroxide. After running an electrolytic cell using this electrode for 110.0 minutes at a current of 0.150 A, the solution in the cathode compartment is analyzed and is found to contain 2.00 x 10^-3 mol H2O2. What is the percent yield of H2O2 based on the current passed through the cell (the faradaic yield)?

What is the Ksp of AgI at 298 K?
Half-reaction | E degrees, V: Ag+(aq) + e- -> Ag(s) = +0.80, AgI(s) + e- -> Ag(s) + I-(aq) = -0.15

In the diagram below, an electrochemical cell's anode and cathode are linked by a semi-permeable membrane that allows only water to pass. The concentrations of Zn(NO3)2(aq) and Cu(NO3)2(aq) are initially each 0.10 M. Which of the following diagrams best shows the water levels in each cell at equilibrium?
choices: Water level higher on Zn side
Water level higher on Cu side
Water level higher on Cu side (more pronounced)

problem: Consider the voltaic cell below at 25 degrees C with 1.00 L of solution in each half-cell. When the switch is closed, the mass of the Cr(s) electrode increases until the cell is fully discharged (i.e., Delta E = 0 V). If the final mass of Cr(s) is 24.50 g, what is Delta E degrees at 25 degrees C for the reaction: 2 Cr3+(aq) + 3 Zn(s) -> 2 Cr(s) + 3 Zn2+(aq)? (Initial Cr electrode: 20.0 g, Initial Zn electrode: 20.0 g, both solutions 0.10 M)
How many orbitals are occupied by at least one electron in the ground state of a gas-phase Si atom?
Which transition of a hydrogen atom would involve absorbing a photon with the longest wavelength?
Which gas-phase ion has the greatest number of unpaired electrons in its ground state?
The ionization energy of which element is closest to that of fluorine (F)?
By what mode does the isotope 79Se undergo radioactive decay?

Despite the fact that atomic radius generally increases with period, the palladium-chlorine distance in PdCl4^2- is essentially identical to the platinum-chlorine distance in PtCl4^2- (2.30 Angstroms). Which is the best explanation of why the two bond lengths are the same?
choices: The presence of the 14 lanthanides between Pd and Pt makes Pt unexpectedly small.
PdCl4^2- is tetrahedral, while PtCl4^2- is square planar and so has shorter bonds than expected.
The metal-chlorine bonds are highly covalent and so are insensitive to the trend in atomic radius.
The large negative charge on the complex ions makes the chloride-chloride repulsions, not the metal-chloride attractions, the dominant factor in determining the bond distance.

problem: Which species has the largest O-N-O bond angle?
Which best describes the isomer of the ion with the formula [CNO]- that is the most stable?
Which is the least important consideration in determining the strength of interaction between atomic orbitals on two atoms as they combine to form molecular orbitals?
choices: How close the atoms are to one another
How close in energy the orbitals are
What the orbitals' relative orientation is
Whether the orbitals have the same principal quantum number

problem: Which statement about FNNF is NOT correct?
choices: It exists as two distinct geometric isomers.
Its nitrogen-nitrogen bond is longer than that in N2F4.
It is more stable than its structural isomer with both fluorine atoms bonded to the same nitrogen.

problem:
A four-coordinate transition metal complex M(CO)2(Br)(Cl) can be isolated as two geometric isomers. With which geometries at the metal center is this observation consistent?
I. Square planar
II. Tetrahedral

A sample of pure Si is doped with 1.0 part per million of As to form material A. Material A is then doped with 0.5 ppm of B to form material B. What is the correct order when the three materials are arranged in order of increasing electrical conductivity?

Which of these could have the formula C6H12O?
I. An acyclic ester
II. A cyclic ether
Which is the best explanation for the higher reactivity of conjugated dienes relative to non-conjugated alkenes in electrophilic addition reactions?
choices: Conjugated dienes can form allylic cations on reaction with electrophiles while non-conjugated alkenes cannot.
Conjugated dienes have more potentially reactive sites than do non-conjugated alkenes.
The pi bonding in conjugated dienes is weaker than the pi bonding in non-conjugated alkenes.
Conjugated dienes are nonplanar while non-conjugated alkenes are planar.

problem: Which compound will NOT form in the reaction of acetone (propanone) with aqueous base?
choices: An aldol product with two OH groups

problem: Which compound would have the lowest C=O stretching frequency in its infrared spectrum?

Which compound reacts fastest with aqueous acid? (Structures shown: A = cyclohexylmethyl alkene, B = cyclohexyl with terminal alkyne, C = cyclohexyl with internal alkyne/allene, D = cyclohexyl with terminal alkyne different position)
How many possible dipeptides can be formed using the twenty commonly occurring amino acids?
Iron(III) sulfide reacts with gaseous hydrogen chloride to produce iron(III) chloride and hydrogen sulfide. How much iron(III) sulfide would be required to react with 1.5 mol HCl?

How much CH4 remains after 10.0 g of CH4 reacts completely with 15.0 g of O2 according to the following equation?
CH4(g) + 2 O2(g) -> CO2(g) + 2 H2O(g)
A sample of 1.00 g of which compound contains the greatest number of atoms?
200.0 mL of aqueous nitric acid is mixed with 300.0 mL of 0.200 M Mg(NO3)2 solution to give a solution with a nitrate ion concentration of 0.500 M. What is the concentration of the nitric acid?

A student wishes to prepare a solution with a final concentration of Na+ = 0.50 M and a final concentration of HCO3- = 0.10 M by taking some NaOH and some trona (Na2CO3*NaHCO3*2H2O) and diluting with water to a final volume of 1.00 L. How much NaOH and trona are required?
choices: A solution cannot be prepared with the desired concentrations of Na+ and HCO3- using only NaOH and trona.

problem:
As the concentration of an aqueous solution of sodium chloride increases from 1.0 M to 2.0 M, the values of which properties of the solution decrease?
I. Density
II. Freezing point
Which ion gives a green flame test?

The equilibrium constant for the formation of CoCl4^2-, a species that is blue in solution, is to be measured using a colorimeter. The colorimeter has wavelength settings of 470 nm ("blue"), 565 nm ("green"), and 635 nm ("red") to use in the experiment. What is the best setting to use?
choices: All settings would be equally suitable for the measurement.

problem: A student is using a coffee-cup calorimeter to determine the enthalpy change of the endothermic reaction of two aqueous solutions. After both solutions are added to the cup, the student neglects to put the lid on the cup. This would cause the magnitude of the calculated Delta H degrees value to be:
choices: too small, since some heat will escape out of the cup.
too large, since some heat will escape out of the cup.
too small, since the solution will absorb heat from the room.
too large, since the solution will absorb heat from the room.

problem: Which substance produces a toxic and explosive gas when added to strong acids?

When aqueous ethanol is treated with an acidified solution of potassium dichromate, what is observed?
I. Color change
II. Gas evolution
Mixing which 0.1 M aqueous solutions results in formation of a colored precipitate?
Which liquid has the highest vapor pressure at 25 degrees C?

A sample of cyclohexane (C6H12, bp = 81 degrees C) is introduced into an evacuated container at 40 degrees C. One-third of the cyclohexane by mass is in the form of the liquid and two-thirds is in the form of the vapor. The volume of the container is then doubled, maintaining the temperature at 40 degrees C. What happens to the pressure as the volume is doubled?
choices: The pressure decreases by a factor of two.

problem: The normal boiling points of ammonia, water, and hydrogen fluoride increase in the order NH3 (bp = -33 degrees C) < HF (bp = 19 degrees C) < H2O (bp = 100 degrees C). What is the best explanation for this ordering?
choices: H2O forms a greater number of hydrogen bonds per mol than NH3 or HF.
H2O forms stronger hydrogen bonds than NH3 or HF.
H2O has a larger dipole moment than NH3 or HF.
H2O has stronger London dispersion forces than NH3 or HF.

problem: Which gas has the smallest molar volume at STP?
Which solid has the highest melting point?
A portion of the structure of solid potassium is shown below. In what type of unit cell are the atoms arranged?

What is the standard enthalpy of formation of C2H2(g)?
C2H2(g) + 5/2 O2(g) -> 2CO2(g) + H2O(l), Delta H degrees_rxn = -1229.6 kJ mol^-1
C(s, graphite) + O2(g) -> CO2(g), Delta H degrees_rxn = -393.5 kJ mol^-1
H2(g) + 1/2 O2(g) -> H2O(l), Delta H degrees_rxn = -285.9 kJ mol^-1
Which reaction occurs with the greatest increase in entropy?

A 2.00 g sample of solid RbClO4 (M = 184.92) is added to 100.0 g water, both initially at 23.00 degrees C, in a well-insulated container. The final temperature of the solution is 21.56 degrees C. What is Delta H degrees_solution of RbClO4? (Assume that the specific heat capacity of the solution is the same as that of pure water and neglect the heat capacity of the insulated container.)

For a reaction studied over a wide temperature range, plots of Delta G degrees_rxn/T vs. 1/T and of Delta G degrees_rxn vs. T are linear. Which of the following must be independent of temperature?
I. Delta G degrees_rxn
II. Delta H degrees_rxn

Elemental tin forms two allotropes, white tin and gray tin. For the conversion of white tin to gray tin, Delta H degrees = -2.02 kJ mol^-1 and Delta S degrees = -7.04 J mol^-1 K^-1. Which statement best describes the stability of the two phases?
choices: White tin is stable below 13.8 degrees C and gray tin is stable above this temperature.
Gray tin is stable below 13.8 degrees C and white tin is stable above this temperature.
White tin is stable below 0.3 degrees C and gray tin is stable above this temperature.
Gray tin is stable below 0.3 degrees C and white tin is stable above this temperature.

problem:
The plot of the natural logarithm of the molar solubility (S) of Ag2SO4 as a function of the reciprocal of the absolute temperature is shown below. The line of best fit is y = -1.2567 - 689.92x. What is Delta H degrees_rxn for the following reaction?
Ag2SO4(s) -> 2 Ag+(aq) + SO4^2-(aq)
A reaction A -> 2 B was monitored over time. What is the average rate of disappearance of A between t = 0 and t = 10 minutes? (Graph shows [A] decreasing from ~0.8 M to ~0.4 M over 0-10 minutes.)

An irreversible reaction A + B -> products is monitored in buffered aqueous solution under conditions where [B] >> [A] by measuring the concentration of A as a function of time. The decay of A is found to be first-order under these conditions, with the half-life for its disappearance varying as shown. What is the rate law for this reaction?
[B]=0.100 M, pH=1.00, half-life=120 s | [B]=0.200 M, pH=1.00, half-life=60 s | [B]=0.200 M, pH=1.50, half-life=190 s

The thermal decomposition of NOCl is a second-order process, and the rate constant k for the disappearance of NOCl at 160 degrees C is 0.0037 M^-1 s^-1. What is the concentration of NOCl, initially at 0.043 M, after 20.0 minutes at 160 degrees C?
Bismuth-212 undergoes both alpha decay (35.9%) and beta decay (64.1%) with an overall half-life of 60.6 minutes. What is its half-life for alpha decay?

A reaction A <=> B is reversible, with Keq = 1.00. The forward reaction is first-order in A. In a reaction initially containing only A at an initial concentration of [A]0, which plot will be linear over the course of the reaction?

The decomposition of nitryl chloride takes place according to the following equation:
2 NO2Cl(g) -> 2 NO2(g) + Cl2(g)
The following mechanism is proposed for this reaction:
NO2Cl(g) <=> NO2(g) + Cl(g)
NO2Cl(g) + Cl(g) -> NO2(g) + Cl2(g)
Which rate laws are INCONSISTENT with the proposed mechanism, regardless of the relative rates of the two steps?
I. Rate = k[NO2Cl]
II. Rate = k[NO2Cl]^2

A sample of SO2Cl2 is introduced into a movable piston with the pressure maintained at 1.00 atm and the temperature at 450 K. The volume increases as the sulfuryl chloride equilibrates according to the following endothermic reaction:
SO2Cl2(g) <=> SO2(g) + Cl2(g)    Delta H degrees > 0
Which changes would decrease the number of moles of SO2Cl2(g) present in the piston at equilibrium? In each case, the pressure is maintained at 1.00 atm.
I. The temperature of the system is increased to 500 K.
II. A sample of Ar(g) is injected into the piston.
Equal volumes of 0.500 M HCO2H and 0.100 M Na(O2CCH3) are mixed. What is the pH of the resulting solution? The pKa of HCO2H is 3.75 and the pKa of CH3CO2H is 4.75.

The formation constant Kf of the hexaamminecadmium ion, Cd(NH3)6^2+, is 2.6 x 10^5. In a solution whose ammonia concentration is 0.12 M, what percentage of the cadmium in solution is in the form of Cd(NH3)6^2+?
The Ksp of Sr(OH)2 is 2.0 x 10^-3. What is the pH of a saturated solution of Sr(OH)2?

When 0.100 M HCl was added to a 10.0 mL sample of aqueous NaOH, it generated the titration curve shown as a solid line. If the same number of moles of NaOH, but initially at a volume of 20.0 mL, were titrated with 0.100 M HCl, which of the dashed curves would best describe this new titration?
choices: Curve A (shifted left, lower initial pH)
Curve B (same equivalence point, lower initial pH)

problem:
A gas X reacts reversibly and independently to form two different gases, Y and Z:
X(g) <=> Y(g)    KY
X(g) <=> Z(g)    KZ
At a certain temperature, the time evolution of the partial pressures of the three gases are shown. What is the equilibrium constant KY at this temperature?
choices: It cannot be determined because the system has not yet reached equilibrium.

problem:
When the following reaction is balanced under basic conditions, what is the ratio of the coefficients of Mn(OH)2(s) to MnO4^2-(aq)?
Mn(OH)2(s) + MnO4-(aq) -> MnO4^2-(aq)

What is the standard reduction potential for the reduction of permanganate ion to manganese dioxide in acidic solution?
Half-Reaction | E degrees, V:
MnO4-(aq) + 8 H+(aq) + 5 e- -> Mn2+(aq) + 4 H2O(l) = +1.51
MnO2(s) + 4 H+(aq) + 2 e- -> Mn2+(aq) + 2 H2O(l) = +1.23
MnO4-(aq) + 4 H+(aq) + 3 e- -> MnO2(s) + 2 H2O(l) = ???
A current of 1.20 A passed through a solution of a metal nitrate for 153 minutes plates out 3.92 g of the metal. What is the metal?

Which changes will lead to an increase in the theoretical cell potential for a hydrogen-oxygen fuel cell?
I. A more efficient catalyst is used at the cathode.
II. The temperature is increased from 298 K to 320 K.
Metallic gold will not dissolve in nitric acid alone, but will dissolve in a mixture of nitric and hydrochloric acids. Which is the best explanation for this observation?
choices: Chloride ion complexes gold(III), stabilizing it as AuCl4-.
Nitric acid is a stronger oxidant in more acidic solution.
Nitric and hydrochloric acids react to form NO2Cl, which reacts readily with metallic gold.
Nitric and hydrochloric acids react to form Cl2, which reacts readily with metallic gold.

problem:
A concentration cell is set up with a metal M and aqueous solutions of the M^n+ ion. The cell potential at 298 K as a function of the logarithm of the [M^n+] in the anode is graphed below. What may be concluded about the value of n?
M(s) | M^n+(aq, variable concentration) || M^n+(aq, 1.00 M) | M(s)
(Graph shows E vs. log[M^n+] with slope approximately -0.030 V per unit)
choices: n cannot be determined from the information given.

problem: A hydrogen atom emits light as it changes from a state with a principal quantum number n+3 to a state with principal quantum number n. Which change causes the greatest change in the energy of the emitted photon?
choices: The initial state is changed to n+4.
The initial state is changed to n+2.
The final state is changed to n+1.
The final state is changed to n-1.

problem: The first three ionization energies (in kJ mol^-1) of four metallic elements are listed below. Which element would form the chloride salt with the lattice energy that is largest in magnitude?
Which element releases the greatest amount of energy upon addition of an electron to a gas-phase atom?
Which species is the strongest oxidant in aqueous solution?
Which gas-phase atom contains the greatest number of unpaired electrons in its ground state?

A clean sample of magnesium metal is irradiated under vacuum with a 1 W laser. If the laser wavelength is 400 nm, nothing happens, but if the wavelength is 300 nm, then electrons with 45.6 kJ mol^-1 of energy are ejected from the Mg surface. What happens if a 2 W laser is used instead?
choices: There is no change at 400 nm, but at 300 nm twice as many electrons are emitted at 45.6 kJ mol^-1.
There is no change at 400 nm, but at 300 nm, electrons are emitted at 91.2 kJ mol^-1.
There is no change at 400 nm, but at 300 nm, electrons are emitted at 444 kJ mol^-1.
At 400 nm, electrons are emitted at 245 kJ mol^-1, while at 300 nm, electrons are emitted at 444 kJ mol^-1.

problem: Which species has the strongest bond?

Which is the most stable arrangement of the atoms in space in formamide, HC(O)NH2? (Four structural diagrams shown with different geometries around nitrogen.)
choices: Planar with trigonal N (all atoms in plane)

problem: Molecular chlorine, Cl2, has a smaller ionization energy than atomic chlorine, Cl (IE = 1108 kJ mol^-1 for Cl2 and 1256 kJ mol^-1 for Cl). Which is the best explanation for this difference?
choices: Cl2 has a covalent bond while Cl does not.
Cl2 has no unpaired electrons while Cl has one unpaired electron.
The electron ionized from Cl2 comes from an orbital that has antibonding character.
The electron ionized from Cl2 comes from an orbital that has d orbital character.

Which compound will NOT react readily in aqueous sodium hydroxide solution? (Four structures shown: A = hydroxyl and methoxy phenol derivative, B = dimethoxy phenol derivative, C = aldehyde, D = ester)
choices: Compound with OH and OCH3 groups

problem: Treatment of acetyl chloride with an equimolar amount of diethylamine at room temperature in diethyl ether gives no more than a 50% yield of N,N-diethylacetamide. Which is the best explanation for this observation?
choices: Half of the diethylamine is consumed by the neutralization of HCl produced in the reaction.
Hydrogen bonding between diethylamine molecules makes the reaction second-order in diethylamine.
Traces of water in the solvent convert at least half of the acetyl chloride into acetic acid.
Diethylamine is a strong base, but a weak nucleophile.
